LAW AND MOTION 302, 1 - DEFENDANT PAUL DICKERSON DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. PAUL DICKERSON'S DEMURRER IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41, WHICH REQUIRES THAT THE PARTIES CONDUCT A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON, BY TELEPHONE, OR BY VIDEO CONFERENCE" BEFORE A DEMURRER IS FILED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY 5/12/2025. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference.
BRAD BYSZEWSKI, ESQ. of VAZIRI LAW GROUP, APC. Tel:(310) 777-7540 for plaintiff.
Akhila Ganapathy, ESQ. of JEANETTE N. LITTLE & ASSOCIATES. Tel: (925) 225-6838 for Defendant.

Hearing is held. Plaintiff's counsel appeared in court to seek clarification on the tentative ruling concerning the filing of the amended complaint and the deadline to file an answer. The matter was discussed but not argued. Pursuant to the court's instruction, the parties are directed to meet and confer. Upon conclusion of the discussion, the court adopted its tentative ruling.

1 - Defendant Paul Dickerson's Demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint for Damages is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order Signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-29-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference.
BRAD BYSZEWSKI, ESQ. of VAZIRI LAW GROUP, APC. Tel:(310) 777-7540 for plaintiff.
Akhila Ganapathy, ESQ. of JEANETTE N. LITTLE & ASSOCIATES. Tel: (925) 225-6838 for Defendant.

Matter not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling.

2 - Defendant Paul Dickerson's Motion to Strike is DENIED.

Defendant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 435. "Any party, within the time allowed to respond to a pleading may serve and file a notice of motion to strike the whole or any part thereof..." (Code of Civil Procedure section 435.) "The court may, upon a motion made pursuant to Section 435, or at any time in its discretion, and upon terms it deems proper: [para] (a) Strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading. (b) Strike out all or any part of any pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (Code of Civil Procedure section 436.)

Here, Defendant's motion fails. Defendant's premise is Plaintiff's Labor Code claim fails; if so, the requests for an attachment and attorney's fees under the Labor Code fail. The court, however, has found Plaintiff has alleged a claim under Labor Code section 3700 et seq. The requests for an attachment and fees are, thus, viable. (See Labor Code sections 3707 and 3709.) Labor Code section 3709 expressly provides for an award of attorney's fees to a prevailing employee in a civil action against an uninsured employer. The court is not persuaded by Defendant's argument (unaccompanied by any citations to authority) regarding "compensation."

Order Signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Off Calendar Apr-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - DEFENDANT PAUL DICKERSON DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Paul Dickerson's demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person, by telephone, or by video conference" before a demurrer is filed. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and Defendant's response to the amended complaint is now due by 5/12/2025.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
